notes: Skill delivers +34% pass-rate uplift (100% vs 66%) — biggest wins are on /craft and /polish where the baseline doesn't know about <StatusChip>, semantic tokens (text-positive vs text-green-500), or that polish should stay non-functional.

Format compliance is the most discriminating assertion: baseline /audit defaults to High/Medium/Low instead of [blocking]/[important]/[nit] and skips the totals-by-severity summary. The skill nails both.

/copy is non-discriminating — both configs score 6/6. The 5 rewrites here may be too obvious; baseline LLM already knows 'sentence case' and 'name the destructive action'. Future evals should include subtler microcopy.

Cost trade-off: +9.6s latency (+18%) and +67% tokens for the skill version. Driven by upfront reference-file loading. /craft is the worst (94s, 36k tokens) — opportunity to make /craft load fewer reference files.

/polish baseline failed the 'non-functional only' assertion by adding role=button, tabIndex, onKeyDown, stopPropagation. The skill's explicit 'non-functional refinements only' framing prevented scope creep — concrete evidence the boundary in SKILL.md earns its keep.
